body:
Markets retreated modestly on Sunday as crypto's total market cap shed 0.60% to $2.61T, with trading volumes hitting $104.7B amid renewed geopolitical tensions. The pullback feels more like position squaring than panic, with most major assets down less than 1% and fear & greed staying entrenched at 29, barely budging from yesterday's 27. The DeFi sector is nursing wounds from the $292M Kelp exploit that's being attributed to North Korea's Lazarus group, part of a broader $13B DeFi wipeout over 48 hours that has developers scrambling after the Vercel hack exposed API keys across the ecosystem.

Bitcoin closed at $74,730, down 0.69% for the session but still holding comfortably above the psychological $74K level that's proven sticky over the past week. The king coin's 5.52% weekly gain remains intact despite today's modest retreat, with dominance steady at 57.4% as money hasn't rotated aggressively into alts. Key support sits at $72K where buyers have repeatedly emerged, while a clean break above $76K would target the $78K-$80K zone that's been our medium-term upside objective. Volume at $41.06B suggests this is more consolidation than distribution, especially with Saylor teasing "bigger" BTC purchases in recent days.

Ethereum underperformed at $2,294.93, dropping 0.99% as the network continues dealing with fallout from the Kelp exploit that highlighted ongoing smart contract risks. ETH's dominance ticked down to 10.6% while trading $17.71B in volume, showing some institutional flows rotating toward Bitcoin's perceived safety. Among majors, BNB bucked the trend with a 0.41% gain to $623.86, while XRP and ETH led declines. Solana held relatively steady at $84.70, down just 0.19% as institutional interest in the Solana ecosystem continues building despite broader DeFi concerns.

The macro backdrop remains dominated by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions that sent oil jumping and risk assets retreating across traditional markets. While crypto has decoupled from some macro correlations, geopolitical flare-ups still trigger risk-off flows that hit digital assets. The DeFi hack cluster has refocused attention on smart contract security just as institutional adoption was gaining momentum, potentially slowing some enterprise deployments. Meanwhile, Polymarket's rumored $400M raise at a $15B valuation signals continued institutional interest in crypto infrastructure despite near-term volatility.

This week brings several key catalysts that could shift momentum either direction. Any escalation in Middle East tensions would likely pressure risk assets further, while de-escalation could unleash pent-up buying pressure. The DeFi exploit aftermath will determine whether this becomes a sector-wide confidence crisis or gets compartmentalized to affected protocols. Watch for Bitcoin to either reclaim $76K on any geopolitical relief or test $72K support if tensions worsen. Ethereum needs to hold $2,200 to maintain its recent recovery narrative, while any fresh DeFi exploits could trigger broader altcoin weakness regardless of Bitcoin's performance.
